title: penstemon clevelandii
intro: Penstemon clevelandii, also known as Cleveland's penstemon, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Penstemon clevelandii include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, or topical salves.
